extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper held his sprint until final 100m while chaos from 4.3km crash scattered favorites, winning with tactical restraint over early-committing rivals.
tactical_patterns: VAN POPPEL Danny sprinted first at 100m mark, forcing ACKERMANN to commit early—leaving space for PHILIPSEN's late jump.
Golden kilometer bonus seconds at three sprints compressed together rewarded aggressive positioning before the decisive late-race crash.
4.3km crash splitting peloton suggests front positioning before final circuit laps is mandatory on narrow wet technical finales.
VAN AERT Wout and LAMPAERT Yves taking bonus seconds in golden kilometer reveals QuickStep willing to burn matches early.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: showed restraint discipline waiting until 100m while rivals committed early in messy sprint
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: marked VAN POPPEL's first jump but lacked acceleration to counter PHILIPSEN's late timing
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: actively contesting golden kilometer bonus seconds suggests competitive sharpness beyond stage hunting
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: neutral: took bonus seconds in golden kilometer but positioning at 4.3km crash suggests misjudged risk window
rider_signals.HAAS Nathan: negative: caught behind 4.3km crash as biggest GC casualty indicates poor peloton positioning anticipation
